q: Where should you begin when tackling a canvas app that is slow to start?
o: Measure the app's load time to find what to optimize
why: You first evaluate/measure load time so optimization targets the real cost.

q: What does showing a loading image at startup accomplish?
o: Improves perceived performance while data loads
why: A loading image improves perceived responsiveness; it doesn't change real load time.

q: Decide whether each statement about startup performance is true.

rows.s: Connector limits and throttling can slow data calls at startup.
Reviewing app settings is part of tuning startup.
Measuring load time is unnecessary before optimizing.

why: Throttling/limits affect startup; you review settings and measure before optimizing.

q: Throttling by a connector means...
o: Requests beyond a rate are delayed or rejected
why: Throttling limits how many calls succeed in a window, delaying/blocking excess requests.

q: Which app setting review most directly affects data correctness on large tables?
why: The data row limit governs how many rows non-delegable queries return.

q: Which preview/experimental settings should you review for performance?
why: Data-loading/delegation settings are the performance-relevant ones to review.

q: Decide whether each statement about app settings is true.

rows.s: The data row limit can be raised to a maximum of 2000.
Lowering the row limit can reduce memory use when appropriate.

why: The limit tops out at 2000; a smaller limit can reduce work if you don't need many rows locally.

q: A slow OnStart is often caused by...
o: Doing too much sequential data work before the first screen shows
why: Heavy sequential OnStart work delays the first render.

q: Which is preferred over setting the app's initial screen with Navigate in OnStart?
o: Set the app's StartScreen property instead of Navigate in OnStart
why: Using StartScreen avoids the overhead/limitations of Navigate during startup.

q: Why move per-screen data loads from App.OnStart into each screen's OnVisible?
o: So each screen only loads its own data when shown, spreading the cost
So the app never loads data
why: OnVisible defers a screen's data work until that screen is actually displayed.

q: Which function loads several independent data sets in parallel to cut startup time?
why: Concurrent runs multiple formulas in parallel rather than one after another.

q: Decide whether each startup-optimization statement is true.

rows.s: OnVisible is better than OnStart for data only one screen needs.
Loading data once and reusing it avoids repeated calls.
Concurrent forces data to load strictly one item at a time.

why: Defer with OnVisible, load once, and use Concurrent to parallelize (not serialize).

q: Which data-load strategy keeps working data in memory to avoid repeated source calls?
o: Direct data source binding on every control
Storing it in the app title
why: Preloading into a collection caches data for fast reuse.

q: 'Direct data source binding' means...
o: A control reads straight from the source each time it needs data
Data is cached in a variable
Data is loaded once at install
why: Direct binding queries the source on demand; good for fresh data, costlier for repeated reads.

q: Which strategy persists data on the device so it survives app restarts?
why: SaveData/LoadData persist data locally across sessions (offline-friendly).

q: Decide whether each data-loading statement is true.

rows.s: Preloading into a variable suits a single reused value.
Direct binding always gives the freshest data but may cost more calls.
Persisted storage is best when data must always be real-time.

why: Variables cache single values; direct binding is fresh but chatty; persisted storage trades freshness for offline availability.

q: How does Monitor help teams debug an app together?
o: You can share a Monitor session so others watch the same live trace
why: Monitor sessions can be shared so collaborators inspect the same live events.

q: A Monitor trace shows an operation taking a long duration. What have you found?
o: A concrete performance hotspot to optimize
why: Duration per event pinpoints the slow operation to fix.

q: Decide whether each Monitor statement is true.

rows.s: Monitor records the sequence and timing of app operations.
Monitor can be used during Studio play, not only in a published app.

why: Monitor captures event order/timing and works in Studio and published apps.

q: Which is a common problem Monitor helps you identify and resolve?
why: Monitor surfaces redundant/slow calls so you can consolidate or cache them.

q: What is Application Insights used for with Power Apps?
o: Collecting telemetry (usage, performance, errors) over time
why: Application Insights (Azure Monitor) captures app telemetry for ongoing diagnostics.

q: How do Monitor and Application Insights differ in scope?
o: Monitor is a live debugging trace; Application Insights logs telemetry over time
why: Monitor is real-time/interactive; Application Insights is longitudinal telemetry.

q: Decide whether each telemetry statement is true.

rows.s: Application Insights can capture unhandled errors from the app.
You connect an app to Application Insights via an instrumentation key/connection string.
Application Insights replaces the need to ever measure load time.

why: App Insights captures errors/telemetry via an instrumentation key but complements, not replaces, load-time analysis.

q: You need historical trends of how many users hit a slow screen last week. Which tool fits?
why: Historical trends are Application Insights' domain; Monitor is for the current session.

q: How can Power BI complement app telemetry?
o: By visualizing Application Insights/usage data in reports
why: Power BI can report on telemetry/usage data to surface trends visually.

q: Which pairing makes the most sense for ongoing app health monitoring?
o: Application Insights feeding Power BI dashboards
why: Telemetry in Application Insights visualized in Power BI gives ongoing health insight.

q: Decide whether each statement is true.

rows.s: Power BI can build dashboards over telemetry exported from Application Insights.
Power BI is where you write the app's Power Fx.

why: Power BI visualizes data (including telemetry); Power Fx is authored in Power Apps.

q: Which end-to-end approach best sustains performance for a growing app?
o: Measure with Monitor, log with Application Insights, and act on the findings
why: Measure (Monitor), log (App Insights) and iterate is the sustainable loop.

q: OnStart sets several global variables the whole app uses. Is that appropriate?
o: Yes for truly app-wide values, but keep OnStart lean
why: App-wide values belong in OnStart, but keep it minimal to protect startup time.

q: A single screen's gallery data is loaded in App.OnStart. Better practice?
o: Move it to that screen's OnVisible
Put it in the app title
Load it in every screen's OnVisible
why: Screen-specific data should load in that screen's OnVisible, not globally at startup.

q: Decide whether each best practice is true.

rows.s: Keeping OnStart minimal improves time-to-first-screen.
Concurrent can parallelize independent OnStart loads.
Setting StartScreen is worse than Navigate in OnStart.

why: Lean OnStart, Concurrent parallelization and StartScreen all help startup.

q: Which report tool is best suited to visualize app usage/telemetry trends for stakeholders?
why: Power BI builds shareable dashboards over telemetry/usage data; Monitor is only a live per-session trace.
